generic.meaning: adj. 一般的;属的;类的;非商标的
example: The novel was criticized for having a generic plot that lacked originality.
The research team had to abandon the project due to a lack of funding.
Over time, toxic chemicals can accumulate in the soil and water supply.
He accumulated a great deal of wealth through years of hard work.

advocate.meaning: v. 提倡，主张，拥护；n. 提倡者
example: Many environmentalists advocate for the immediate reduction of carbon emissions.

ambiguous.meaning: adj. 模棱两可的，含糊不清的
example: The instructions were so ambiguous that nobody knew what to do next.
Economists anticipate that the unemployment rate will drop next year.

bias.meaning: n. 偏见，偏心；v. 使有偏见
example: The study was criticized because the sampling method introduced a strong bias.

cohesive.meaning: adj. 凝聚的，有凝聚力的，团结的
example: A cohesive essay requires clear transition words between paragraphs.
The company agreed to compensate the consumers for the defective products.
The exam will comprise a listening section, a reading section, and an essay.
A quiet environment is highly conducive to deep learning and study.

contradict.meaning: v. 反驳，与…矛盾，相抵触
example: The new statistical evidence seems to contradict the previous hypothesis.
Research shows a strong correlation between poverty and low educational output.
The availability of natural resources will diminish rapidly if waste continues.

empirical.meaning: adj. 以经验为依据的，凭经验的，实证的
example: The scientists provided empirical data to support their revolutionary theory.
Using visuals in a presentation can significantly enhance student engagement.

equate.meaning: v. 等同，使相等，把…视为同等
example: You cannot simply equate high income with a high quality of life.
Frequent heavy rains can erode the soil on the mountainside.
The price of oil tends to fluctuate wildly based on global political events.
The school aims to foster independent thinking skills among its students.
There is a rigid social hierarchy in many traditional corporate cultures.
The university scholars conducted an experiment to test their hypothesis.
Tax breaks provide a strong financial incentive for companies to go green.

indispensable.meaning: adj. 不可或缺的，绝对必要的
example: Computers have become an indispensable tool in modern academic research.

inevitable.meaning: adj. 不不可避免的，必然发生的
example: With the rise of automation, some job losses in manufacturing are inevitable.

inherent.meaning: adj. 固有的，内在的，天生的
example: Every scientific exploration carries an inherent risk of failure.
The company won an award for its innovative approach to waste management.

insights.meaning: n. 洞察力，深刻见解（复数）
example: The book provides valuable insights into the psychology of consumer behavior.
It is important to integrate technology effectively into the classroom curriculum.
The minister was asked to justify the massive increase in public spending.

marginal.meaning: adj. 微小的，少量的，边缘的
example: The improvement in the student's test scores was marginal at best.
We may need to modify the experimental design based on these initial results.
The report challenges the traditional notion that modern cities are isolating.

objective.meaning: adj. 客观的；n. 目标，目的
example: An investigator must remain completely objective when analyzing the evidence.

paradox.meaning: n. 自相矛盾的人或事，悖论
example: It is a paradox that some of the world's richest countries have high poverty rates.
Traveling broadens the mind and gives you a completely new perspective on life.
Global warming is a complex phenomenon that affects weather patterns worldwide.
Obesity has become increasingly prevalent in developed nations over the last decade.

radical.meaning: adj. 根本的，激进的，彻底的
example: The local government proposed a radical reform of the entire healthcare system.
The positive feedback helped to reinforce the child's good behavior.
Many consumers are reluctant to buy electric cars due to battery range anxiety.
The scarcity of clean drinking water is a major crisis in arid regions.

scrutinize.meaning: v. 仔细检查，认真审查
example: The committee will closely scrutinize every detail of the proposed budget.

simultaneous.meaning: adj. 同时发生的，同时存在的
example: The concert was broadcast with simultaneous translation in five languages.
The first study was flawed, but subsequent research confirmed the initial findings.

sustainable.meaning: adj. 可持续的，可维持的
example: The country is trying to find sustainable alternatives to fossil fuels.
Synthetic materials like plastic have replaced natural substances in many fields.

tangible.meaning: adj. 有形的，切实的，可感知的
example: The new policy has produced tangible benefits for low-income families.

trigger.meaning: v. 引发，触发；n. 起因，扳机
example: A sudden drop in temperature can trigger severe symptoms in asthma patients.

unprecedented.meaning: adj. 前所未有的，空前的
example: The island experienced an unprecedented level of rainfall during the storm.

valid.meaning: adj. 有效的，正当的，有根据的
example: You need to present a valid reason if you want to request an extension.
